ground_truth.title: Best Online Certificate Programs
ground_truth.author: Alison Plaut
ground_truth.main_content: Whether you’re eager to boost your skills for a new career or level up in your current one, certificate programs are a quick and affordable way to do so. They offer training in many fields, including healthcare and technology. An online format means you can earn these credentials without sacrificing your job or other personal obligations. Plus, certificates cost less than a four-year degree, and the credits may transfer to an undergraduate or graduate program. Explore some of the best online certificate programs to see what’s right for you. Why Earn a Certificate Online? Learn specialized skills in just 3-12 months — much faster than a bachelor’s program. Juggle school, work, and other commitments more easily with the flexibility of online learning. Save money on transportation, relocation, and other expenses you would incur with a traditional program. Upskill in your field, or pivot to a new career. Top Accredited Schools Offering Online Certificate Programs We use trusted sources like Peterson's Data and the National Center for Education Statistics to inform the data for these schools. Sponsored results from our partners appear among these rankings and are indicated as such. #1 Southern New Hampshire University Manchester, NH 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School Southern New Hampshire University offers four undergraduate and 19 graduate certificate programs online. These programs span fields such as marketing, human resource management, and computer programming. SNHU's certificates are career-focused and designed to enhance existing degrees. Highlights Complete a certificate program in as few as four months. Access academic and career advising, tutoring, and tech support. Receive accommodations for disabilities. Enjoy discounts for active-duty military and their spouses. Know Before You Apply Some certificate programs may not qualify for financial aid. Graduate certificates require a minimum 2.75 GPA for full acceptance or a 2.0 for provisional acceptance. #2 Ohio State University Columbus, OH 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School You can earn one of nine undergraduate and 20 graduate certificates online at The Ohio State University. These programs cater to various academic interests and professional goals, such as diversity, equity, and inclusion; nursing in school health services; and artificial intelligence in digital health. Highlights Earn some certificates after completing only four courses. Access a 360-degree virtual lab for select certificates. Some certificates, like healthcare revenue cycle and data analytics, can lead to licensure. Know Before You Apply Ohio State's certificate programs are primarily offered asynchronously, meaning you'll need to follow your own schedule to complete coursework. Students need a minimum 2.0 GPA in postsecondary coursework for admission to undergraduate certificate programs. #3 University of Virginia Charlottesville, VA 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School The University of Virginia offers four undergraduate and 10 graduate certificates via distance education for working adults. These programs cater to different career and education levels. They cover fields such as cloud computing, project management, and information technology. Highlights Apply in summer, fall, or spring. Boost your chances of securing academic jobs with a recognized credential in a related field. Pay the same tuition as in-state or out-of-state students. Know Before You Apply Some programs have only synchronous classes, while others mix class formats. Lengths of programs and completion times vary. #4 University of Florida Gainesville, FL 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School The University of Florida stands out for its diverse online certificate options, with 98 graduate and 12 undergraduate programs. These certificates cover fields such as medicine, engineering, agriculture, arts, and education. Highlights Choose from more than 100 options to find your ideal certificate. Enroll as a non-degree-seeking student or a current UF student. Apply graduate certificate credits toward a master's degree. Know Before You Apply Credits from some UF certificate courses may or may not transfer to advanced degrees. Out-of-state students might face higher tuition in some programs. Non-degree students may need an associate degree in a related field for some undergraduate certificate programs. #5 North Carolina State University Raleigh, NC 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School At North Carolina State University, you can earn one of 18 undergraduate and 47 graduate certificate programs fully online in fields such as engineering, business, and the sciences. Unique options include 5G technology, climate adaptation, and crop science. Highlights Retool or advance your career with a plethora of certificate options. Enjoy flexible scheduling to complete your coursework. Choose fully online classes or a mix of online and in-person courses. Know Before You Apply Courses vary in format, including live and self-paced options. Non-degree students usually can't get financial aid, with the exception of some graduate certificates. #6 Boston University Boston, MA 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School Boston University offers 36 online graduate certificates in fields such as art education, crime analysis, and financial management. Aimed at professionals who hold undergraduate degrees, these programs focus on practical skills to enhance career prospects. Highlights Finish your certificate in under a year. Study anytime with 24-7 access to course materials. Access financial aid, including scholarships, loans, and payment plans. Know Before You Apply Certificate costs vary by program and course. No online undergraduate certificates are available. All BU graduate certificate programs require an undergraduate degree for admission. #7 Texas A & M University-College Station College Station, TX 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School Texas A&M University has more than 60 online certificate programs geared toward working professionals and current students. These programs cover architecture, engineering, medicine, and more. Unique options include certificates in circuit design, safety engineering, and geographic information science. Highlights Complete some programs in just one year. Apply certificate credits toward a degree at Texas A&M. Choose areas of emphasis for certain certificate programs. Know Before You Apply Some certificates have limited start dates. Some in-person requirements may be necessary for certain certificate programs. #8 University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill Chapel Hill, NC 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School The University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill offers 10 online undergraduate and graduate certificate programs in business, journalism, law, public health, and more. These programs are ideal for working professionals aiming to advance their careers or pursue a degree. Highlights Gain admission with no test or degree requirements for some certificates. Complete some programs in just six months. Pause courses and finish within 18 months. Apply up to 40% of certificate credits toward a degree. Know Before You Apply Some programs accept only one cohort each year. Requirements for some certificate programs include a graduate school application and personal essay. All graduate certificates require GMAT or GRE scores for admission, but waivers are available. #9 University of Georgia Athens, GA 4 year Campus + Online Accredited Why We Chose This School The University of Georgia has 22 online undergraduate and graduate certificate programs in fields including education, leadership, law, and science. Notably, these offerings include interdisciplinary qualitative sciences and international biomedical regulatory sciences. Highlights Choose from a plethora of teacher- and educator-friendly certificates. Receive financial assistance as an active-duty military service member, veteran, military dependent, or other qualifying professional. Pursue a UGA certificate to start or advance your career or switch careers. Know Before You Apply Some programs have limited application dates. Non-UGA students need to be accepted into the graduate school first to pursue graduate certificates.
with: Whether you’re eager to boost your skills for a new career or level up in your current one, certificate programs are a quick and affordable way to do so.
An online format means you can earn these credentials without sacrificing your job or other personal obligations.
Southern New Hampshire University offers four undergraduate and 19 graduate certificate programs online.
The University of Florida stands out for its diverse online certificate options, with 98 graduate and 12 undergraduate programs.
Boston University offers 36 online graduate certificates in fields such as art education, crime analysis, and financial management.
